{{Short description|none}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Guyana women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                  = Guyana
| Badge                 = Flag of Guyana.svg
| Badge_size            = 200px
| Nickname              = ''Golden Jaguars''
| Association           = [[Guyana Football Federation]] (GFF)
| Confederation         = [[CONCACAF]] (North America)
| Sub-confederation     = [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] (Caribbean)
| Coach                 = [[Marco Bonofiglio]]
| Captain               = [[Daniel Wilson (footballer, born 1993)|Daniel Wilson]]
| Most caps             = [[Walter Moore (footballer, born 1984)|Walter Moore]] (77)
| Top scorer            = [[Omari Glasgow]] (23) 
| Home Stadium          = [[Providence Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme        = GUY
| FIFA Rank             = {{FIFA World Rankings|GUY}}
| FIFA max              = 86
| FIFA max date         = November 2010
| FIFA min              = 185
| FIFA min date         = February 2004
| Elo Rank              = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Guyana}}
| Elo max               = 86
| Elo max date          = April 1980 <!-- Highest rating (1405); in the 1930s Guyana reached rankings between 52 and 54, exclusively playing the teams of Trinidad and Barbados -->
| Elo min               = 183
| Elo min date          = April 1996
| pattern_la1           = _guy25h
| pattern_b1            = _guy25h
| pattern_ra1           = _guy25h
| pattern_sh1           = _guy25h
| pattern_so1           = 
| leftarm1              = FEED39
| body1                 = FEED39
| rightarm1             = FEED39
| shorts1               = 27B14C
| socks1                = ff0000
| pattern_la2           = _guy25a
| pattern_b2            = _guy25a
| pattern_ra2           = _guy25a
| pattern_sh2           = _guy25a
| pattern_so2           = 
| leftarm2              = 27B14C
| body2                 = 27B14C
| rightarm2             = 27B14C
| shorts2               = 27B14C
| socks2                = ffffff
| First game            = '''''as British Guiana'''''<br>{{fb|British Guiana}} 1–4 {{fb-rt|TRI|colonial}}<br>([[British Guiana]]; 21 July 1905)<ref name="listofmatches">{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablest/trin-intres.html|title=Trinidad and Tobago – List of International Matches|website=[[RSSSF|Rsssf.com]]|access-date=3 February 2023|archive-date=2 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221202205908/https://www.rsssf.org/tablest/trin-intres.html|url-status=live}}</ref><br>'''''as Guyana'''''<br>{{fb-rt|SUR}} 4–1 {{fb|GUY}}<br>([[Suriname]]; 1 October 1971)
| Largest win           = '''''as British Guiana'''''<br>{{fb-rt|British Guiana}} 9–0 {{fb|BRB|1885}}<br>([[British Guiana]]; 13 March 1931)<br>'''''as Guyana'''''<br>{{fb|GUY}} 14–0 {{fb-rt|AIA}}<br>([[St. John's, Antigua and Barbuda]]; 16 April 1998)
| Largest loss          = '''''as British Guiana'''''<br>{{flagicon|NED}} [[Suriname national football team|Suriname]] 9–0 {{fb-rt|British Guiana}}<br>([[Netherlands Antilles]]; 17 February 1952)<br> {{flagicon|NED}} [[Suriname national football team|Suriname]] 9–0 {{fb-rt|British Guiana}}<br>([[Aruba]]; 9 February 1953)<br>'''''as Guyana'''''<br>{{fb|GUY}} 0–9 {{fb-rt|MEX}}<br>([[Santa Ana, California|Santa Ana]], [[United States]]; 2 December 1987)
| Regional name         = [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]]
| Regional cup apps     = 1 
| Regional cup first    = [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]]
| Regional cup best     = Group stage ([[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]])
|pattern_la3=_guy25t|pattern_b3=_guy25t|pattern_ra3=_guy25t|socks3=fffff|shorts3=fffff|rightarm3=fffff|body3=fffff|leftarm3=fffff}}

The '''Guyana national football team''' represents [[Guyana]] in men's international [[Association football|football]], which is governed by the [[Guyana Football Federation]] founded in 1902. It has been an affiliate member of [[FIFA]] since 1968, and although it is a country geographically located in [[South America]], it has been an affiliate member of [[CONCACAF]] since 1961 (as [[British Guiana]] until its independence in 1966). Regionally, it is an affiliate member of [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] in the Caribbean Zone.

Guyana has never participated in the [[FIFA World Cup]], but has qualified once for the [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]] in [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]], and has also participated once in League A and three times in League B of the [[CONCACAF Nations League]].
Regionally, the team finished fourth place in the [[1991 Caribbean Cup]].

== History ==
=== British Guiana (1905–1966) ===
Guyana (then British Guiana) played its first international football match on 21 July 1905, a 4–1 defeat against neighboring British colony [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]]. Its next match was almost 16 years later, on 28 January 1921, a 2–1 away victory against [[Suriname national football team|Suriname]]. The two teams met again in Suriname on 27 August 1923, in the [[Martinez Shield]], and this time the home side won 2–1. British Guiana regularly participated in the Martinez Shield between 1923 and 1933, winning the tournament in 1929, 1931, and 1932.<ref name="Martinez Shield">{{cite web|title=Martinez Shield|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/martinez.html|website=Rsssf.com|access-date=18 November 2025|author=Barrie Courtney and Neil Morrison|date=5 November 2020}}</ref>

It played two more matches against Trinidad and Tobago in Suriname in 1937, both resulting in defeats: 3–0 and 3–2. In 1944, British Guiana participated in a triangular tournament in Trinidad and Tobago, facing its national team and [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]]. It won two matches against Barbados (1–0 and 3–0) before drawing 1–1 and losing 3–0 to Trinidad and Tobago. In the final of this triangular tournament, it again lost 3–0 to Trinidad and Tobago.

In November 1947 British Guiana played in a [[Standard Life]] tournament in Trinidad and Tobago.<ref>{{cite web|title=Standard Life Trophy 1947|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/standardlife47.html|website=Rsssf.com|access-date=18 November 2025|author=Barrie Courtney and Neil Morrison|date=19 November 2020}}</ref> They beat the hosts 2–1 in their opening game on 5 November before beating [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]] 2–0 the very next day. On 10 November they drew 0–0 with Jamaica before losing 2–0 to Trinidad and Tobago in the last game on 14 November.

British Guiana played its first home games in 1950 against Trinidad and Tobago: these were British Guiana's first matches since the Standard Life tournament. British Guiana lost 1–0 and 4–1 before winning 1–0. The last match played under the name British Guiana was the 1–1 draw against Barbados in January 1966.<ref>{{cite web|title=Barbados - List of International Matches|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesb/barb-intres.html|website=Rsssf.com|access-date=18 November 2025|author=Barrie Courtney|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

=== 1966–2006 ===
After gaining independence in 1966, Guyana played its first official competitive matches during the qualifiers for the [[1971 CONCACAF Championship]] against [[Suriname (Kingdom of the Netherlands)|Suriname]]. The first match, away, was lost 4–1 and the home match on 21 September 1971 was lost 3–2 as Suriname advanced 7–3 on aggregate. In 1976 Guyana entered its first ever World Cup qualification campaign with the aim of reaching the [[1978 FIFA World Cup]] in Argentina. Guyana and Suriname were drawn in a two-legged preliminary in the Caribbean section of CONCACAF qualification and Guyana won the first leg 2–0 at home on 4 July 1976. The second leg in [[Paramaribo]] was lost 3–0 which allowed Suriname to advance.

Guyana participated in its first [[Caribbean Cup]] finals in [[1991 Caribbean Cup|1991]], where it finished in 4th place. But the 2000s got off to a bad start with a FIFA suspension which prevented him from participating in the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|qualifiers for the 2002 World Cup]].

Guyana had a remarkable calendar year in 2006, with eleven successive wins, including five [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]] qualifiers.<ref>{{cite web
| url= https://www.fifa.com/associations/association=guy/fixturesresults/gender=m/index.html| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070630151606/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=guy/fixturesresults/gender=m/index.html| url-status = dead| archive-date = 30 June 2007| publisher  = [[FIFA]]| title = Guyana: Fixtures and Results| access-date = 16 July 2010}}</ref> These results boosted Guyana's spot in the [[FIFA World Rankings]] by 87 spots in little over a year. As a consequence, the team rose to the top 12 in CONCACAF and were in the third rank of seeds in the World Cup qualifying draw.

=== 2006–2019 ===
==== Caribbean Nations Cup 2007 and 2010 ====
At the [[2006–07 Caribbean Nations Cup]], Guyana finished top of Group A in Stage One, then top of Group H in Stage Two (which they hosted), and finished 3rd in the Bobby Sookram Group, missing out on a semi-final berth on goal difference alone. Had Guyana reached the semi-finals, they would have qualified for the [[2007 CONCACAF Gold Cup]].

Having qualified for the final phase of the [[2010 Caribbean Cup]], held in [[Martinique]], Guyana finished last in Group I with one point from a 1–1 draw with [[Guadeloupe national football team|Guadeloupe]].

==== 2014 World Cup qualifying ====
With the return of international coach [[Jamaal Shabazz]], Guyana finished top of a group containing [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]], [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] and [[Bermuda national football team|Bermuda]] to reach the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|third round]] of qualifying for the [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014 World Cup]] in Brazil. They qualified  with one game to go with a 2–1 home win against Trinidad and Tobago on 11 November 2011.

Guyana organised friendly matches in 2012 against [[Panama national football team|Panama]] (20 May), [[Colombia national football team|Colombia]] (28 May) and [[Bolivia national football team|Bolivia]] (15 August) for the first team. In the third round group, they finished last, behind [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]], [[Costa Rica national football team|Costa Rica]] and [[El Salvador national football team|El Salvador]], with one point from their six matches.

==== Lack of football 2013/14 ====
From November 2012 to October 2014 Guyana did not play a single international fixture. This amongst other factors led to [[FIFA]] stepping in and removing the [[Guyana Football Federation|GFF executive]] at the end of 2014.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.worldsoccer.com/blogs/brief-history-football-guyana-356619/|title=A brief history of football in Guyana|date=20 September 2014|website=Worldsoccer.com|access-date=23 February 2015|archive-date=24 February 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150224055504/http://www.worldsoccer.com/blogs/brief-history-football-guyana-356619|url-status=live}}</ref>

==== 2015 and 2016 return of the Golden Jaguars ====
With FIFA stepping into Guyana once again, a FIFA Normalisation Committee was installed to regularise football in Guyana. With this came the search for a National Team Head Coach with [[Jamaal Shabazz]] reinstalled initially for one game versus [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] on February 2, 2015. As Guyana had lost many first team players to retirement since 2012, the squad was a new younger group with major gaps in the goalkeeper and defensive areas.

==== First CONCACAF Gold Cup in 2019 ====
Having finished 7th in the [[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League qualifying#Standings|2019–2020 CONCACAF Nations League qualifiers]], the Guyanese team have earned the right to participate for the first time in the [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]] in the [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019 edition]].

Placed in Group D of the tournament alongside the host country (the [[United States men's national soccer team|United States]]), [[Panama national football team|Panama]] and [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]], Guyana was eliminated in the first round with only one draw (1–1 against Trinidad and Tobago).

=== 2020&minus;present ===
During the qualifying tournaments for the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)#First round|2022]] and [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)#Second round|2026]] World Cups, Guyana was quickly eliminated (1st round and 2nd round in 2022 and 2026 respectively).

Promoted to [[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League A|League A]] of the [[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League]], the ''Golden Jaguars'' were immediately relegated. They missed the opportunity to compete in the [[2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup]], being eliminated in the [[2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup qualification|prelims]] by [[Guatemala national football team|Guatemala]] in two legs (3–2 and 0–2).

==Team image==
===Kit sponsorship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left;"
|-
! Kit supplier
! Period
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Uhlsport]] || 2002–2004
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]] || 2005–2007
|-
| None (In House)|| 2008–2010
|-
| {{flagicon|HAI}} Plus One || 2011
|-
| {{flagicon|COL}} [[Saeta (brand)|Saeta]] || 2012–2015
|-
| {{flagicon|UK}} [[Admiral Sportswear|Admiral]] || 2016
|-
| {{flagicon|JPN}} Squadra || 2018
|-
| {{flagicon|USA}} [[Capelli Sport]] || 2019–2023
|-
| {{flagicon|USA}} SQ Apparel || 2024
|-
| {{flagicon|SPA}} [[Meyba]] || 2025–present
|}

==Results and fixtures==
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 15 November 
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GUY}}
|score      = 2–1
|team2      = {{fb|BOE}}
|report     = [https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1FtJuDqpfp/?mibextid=wwXIfr Report (GFA)]<br>[https://www.facebook.com/share/p/17Q2efxKdE/?mibextid=wwXIfr Report (BFF)]
|goals1     =
*[[Stephen Duke-McKenna|Duke-McKenna]] {{goal|5}}
*[[Osaze De Rosario|De Rosario]] {{goal|77}}
|goals2     =
*[[Ronalchio Clijdesdale|Clijdesdale]] {{goal|16}}
|location   = [[North Sound]], Antigua and Barbuda
|stadium    = [[Sir Vivian Richards Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    =
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 18 November 
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ATG}}
|score      = 1–4  
|team2      = {{fb|GUY}}
|report     = https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c3rj0elq2n7t
|goals1     =
*[[Quinton Griffith|Griffith]] {{goal|61|pen.}}
|goals2     =
*[[Omari Glasgow|Glasgow]] {{goal|12||23|pen.}}
*[[Osaze De Rosario|De Rosario]] {{goal|71}}
*[[Stephen Duke-McKenna|Duke-McKenna]] {{goal|76}}
|location   = [[North Sound]], Antigua and Barbuda
|stadium    = [[Sir Vivian Richards Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Andrew Samuel ([[Trinidad and Tobago Football Association|Trinidad and Tobago]])
|result     = W
}}

===2026===
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = March 27
|time       = {{UTZ|16:30|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GUY}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|DMA}}
|report     = https://www.concacaf.com/concacaf-series/news/amatkarijo-de-rosario-score-braces-in-concacaf-series/
|goals1     = *[[Osaze De Rosario|De Rosario]] {{goal|28||31}}
|goals2     =
|location   = [[Santiago de los Caballeros]], Dominican Republic 
|stadium    = [[Estadio Cibao FC]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Patrick Senecharles]] ([[Haitian Football Federation|Haiti]])
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = March 30
|time       = {{UTZ|16:30|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GUY}}
|score      = 3–1
|team2      = {{fb|BLZ}}
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-30/guyana-vs-belize/4095374
|goals1     = *[[Osaze De Rosario|De Rosario]] {{goal|2||22||90+1}}
|goals2     = *[[Desmond Wade]] {{goal|8}}
|location   = [[Santiago de los Caballeros]], Dominican Republic 
|stadium    = [[Estadio Cibao FC]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Eric de la Cruz ([[Football Association of Cuba|Cuba]])
|result     = W

}}

== Coaching staff ==
''{{As of|2026|07|11}}''

{| class="wikitable"
|-
| Head coach
| {{flagicon|CAN}} [[Marco Bonofiglio]]<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.guyanafootball.org/gff-appoints-marco-bonofiglio-as-golden-jaguars-head-coach/ |title=GFF Appoints Marco Bonofiglio as Golden Jaguars Head Coach |date=2026-07-11 |website=guyanafootball.org |access-date=2026-07-11 |language=en}}</ref>
|-

| Assistant coach
| {{flagicon|GUY}} [[Sam Cox (footballer, born 1990)|Samuel Cox]]
|-
| Strength & Conditioning Coach
| {{flagicon|GUY}} [[Renzo Patel]]
|-
| Team Manager
| {{flagicon|GUY}} [[Tyrese Stanley]]
|-
| Goalkeeping coach
| {{flagicon|GUY}} [[Evon Cobaine]]
|-
| Kitman
| {{flagicon|GUY}} [[Hal Portius]]
|- 
| Head scout
| {{flagicon|GUY}} [[Romild Christie]]
|-
|GFF President
|{{flagicon|Guyana}} [[Wayne Forde]]<ref>[http://guyanachronicle.com/wayne-forde-guyanas-new-football-boss-team-integrity-triumphs/]{{dead link|date=November 2018}}</ref>
|-
| Technical director
| {{flagicon|GUY}} [[Gennarius Huxley]]
|}

===Coaching history===

{{columns-list|colwidth=22em|
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Feroze Usman]] (1975–82)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Mervyn Wilson]] (1983)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Lennox Arthur]] (1984–1987)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Mervyn Wilson]] (1988)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Gordon Braithwaite]] (1991–1992)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Mervyn Wilson]] (1993–1994)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Earl O'Neal]] (1996)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Deryck White]] (1998)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Joseph Wilson (football manager)|Joseph Wilson]] (2000–2002)
*{{Flagicon|Brazil}} [[Neider dos Santos]] (2002–2004)
*{{Flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[:en:Jamaal Shabazz|Jamaal Shabazz]] (2005–2009)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Wayne Dover]] (2009–2010)
*{{Flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[:en:Jamaal Shabazz|Jamaal Shabazz]] (2011–2012)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Denzil Thompson]] (2014)
*{{Flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[:en:Jamaal Shabazz|Jamaal Shabazz]] (2015–2016)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Wayne Dover]] (2017) 
*{{flagicon|Jamaica}} [[Michael Johnson (footballer, born 1973)|Michael Johnson]] (2018–2019)
*{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[Márcio Máximo]] (2019–2021)
*{{Flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[:en:Jamaal Shabazz|Jamaal Shabazz]] (2021–2024)
*{{Flagicon|Guyana}} [[Wayne Dover]] (2024–2025)
*{{Flagicon|USA}} [[Thomas Dooley]] (2025–2026)
*{{Flagicon|CAN}} [[Marco Bonofiglio]] (2026-)
}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the 2026–27 CONCACAF Series matches against [[Dominica national football team|Dominica]] and [[Belize national football team|Belize]] on 27 and 31 March 2026; respectively.<ref>{{cite web |title=SQUAD LIST {{!}} Guyana Golden Jaguars |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=953371180715582&locale=en_GB |website=Facebook |access-date=26 March 2026 |publisher=Guyana Football Federation |date=21 March 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals updated as of 30 March 2026, after the match against [[Belize national football team|Belize]]''.

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Akel Clarke]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1988|10|25}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Joshua Naraine|age={{birth date and age|2003|01|22|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Pickering FC|Pickering]]|clubnat=CAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Grant Wyles|age={{birth date and age|2005|01|03|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Niagara Purple Eagles men's soccer|Niagara Purple Eagles]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Liam Gordon (footballer, born 1999)|Liam Gordon]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1999|5|15}}|caps=28|goals=2|club=[[Port Vale F.C.|Port Vale]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jalen Jones (footballer)|Jalen Jones]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1998|11|13}}|caps=26|goals=2|club=[[Dover Athletic F.C.|Dover Athletic]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Terence Vancooten]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|12|29|df=yes}}|caps=20|goals=1|club=[[Burton Albion F.C.|Burton Albion]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=Kvist Paul|age={{birth date and age|2006|04|01|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Waterhouse F.C.|Waterhouse]]|clubnat=JAM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Walker Shabazz-Edwards]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|05|04|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Ipswich Town F.C.|Ipswich Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kelsey Benjamin]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1999|5|8}}|caps=39|goals=7|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nathan Moriah-Welsh]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2002|3|18}}|caps=30|goals=3|club=[[Mansfield Town F.C.|Mansfield Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Elliot Bonds]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2000|03|23}}|caps=27|goals=0|club=[[Fleetwood Town F.C.|Fleetwood Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Curtez Kellman]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|03|06|df=yes}}|caps=22|goals=0|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nathan Ferguson (footballer, born 1995)|Nathan Ferguson]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1995|10|12}}|caps=15|goals=1|club=[[Hartlepool United F.C.|Hartlepool United]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Raushan Ritch|age={{birth date and age|2000|04|05|df=yes}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kyle Reid]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|01|03|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Shemar Scott|age={{birth date and age|2000|09|27|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Guyana Defence Force FC|Guyana Defence Force]]|clubnat=GUY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Brandon Solomon|age={{birth date and age|2004|04|04|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Guyana)|Police]]|clubnat=GUY}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Omari Glasgow]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2003|11|22}}|caps=37|goals=23|club=[[Monterey Bay FC|Monterey Bay]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Osaze De Rosario]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2001|7|19}}|caps=13|goals=13|club=[[Seattle Sounders FC|Seattle Sounders]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Enoch George (footballer)|Enoch George]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2003|5|23}}|caps=6|goals=1|club=[[SVV Scheveningen|Scheveningen]]|clubnat=NED}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Maliq Cadogan]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2004|2|25}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Peterborough Sports F.C.|Peterborough Sports]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Reiss Elliott-Parris]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|11|04|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Tottenham Hotspur F.C.|Tottenham Hotspur]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=Bryan Wharton|age={{birth date and age|2007|08|28|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up within the past year.
<!--Sorted by position, most recent call-up, caps, goals and last name.-->
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Quillan Roberts]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1994|9|13}}|caps=24|goals=0|club=[[Western Suburbs FC|Western Suburbs]]|clubnat=NZL|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Kai McKenzie-Lyle]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1997|11|30}}|caps=14|goals=1|club=[[FC Ferizaj|Ferizaj]]|clubnat=KOS|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Leo Lovell|age={{bda|df=yes|1996|12|6}}|caps=29|goals=2|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=3|pos=DF|name=Romaine Brackenbridge|age={{bda|df=yes|1994|9|13}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Marcus Wilson (footballer)|Marcus Wilson]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2002|4|19}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Colin Nelson (footballer, born 1991)|Colin Nelson]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1991|8|9}}|caps=36|goals=1|club=[[Guyana Defence Force FC|Guyana Defence Force]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Reiss Greenidge]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1996|8|10}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Maidstone United F.C.|Maidstone United]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Stephen Duke-McKenna]]|age={{bda|df=yes|2000|8|17}}|caps=30|goals=4|club=[[Harrogate Town A.F.C.|Harrogate Town]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Ryan Hackett|age={{birth date and age|1999|09|11|df=yes}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Guyana)|Police]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Darron Niles|age={{bda|df=yes|2003|5|3}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Slingerz FC|Slingerz]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Neron Barrow|age={{birth date and age|2004|03|14|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Guyana)|Police]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Daniel Wilson (footballer, born 1993)|Daniel Wilson]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1993|11|1}}|caps=66|goals=1|club=[[Western Tigers FC|Western Tigers]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nicholas McArthur]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|12|21|df=yes}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Police F.C. (Guyana)|Guyana Police]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Deon Moore]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|05|14|df=yes}}|caps=15|goals=5|club=[[Maidstone United F.C.|Maidstone United]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Liam Butts]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|02|20|df=yes}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Atlanta United 2]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|ATG}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Chris Macey|age={{birth date and age|1999|12|16|df=yes}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Guyana Defence Force FC|Guyana Defence Force]]|clubnat=GUY|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>INJ</sup> Withdrew due to injury.<br/>
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br/>
<sup>RET</sup> Retired from the national team.<br/>
<sup>WD</sup> Withdrew for personal reasons.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Guyana.''
{{updated|30 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Guyana |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/80/Guyana.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=8 August 2022 |archive-date=6 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230306180610/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/80/2023/Guyana.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

===Most appearances===
[[File:Walter Moore 1.jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Walter Moore (footballer, born 1984)|Walter Moore]] is Guyana's most capped player with 77 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Walter Moore (footballer, born 1984)|Walter Moore]]
|77
|5
|2004–2019
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Daniel Wilson (footballer, born 1993)|Daniel Wilson]]'''
|70
|1
|2011–present
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Earl O'Neal]]
|69
|1
|1971–1980
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Charles Pollard (footballer)|Charles Pollard]]
|60
|3
|1996–2012
|-
|5
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Anthony Abrams]]
|58
|15
|2004–2017
|-
|6
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Trayon Bobb]]
|51
|12
|2011–2023
|-
|rowspan=2|7
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Howard Lowe]]
|46
|1
|2002–2010
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Gregory Richardson]]
|46
|17
|2002–2019
|-
|9
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Dwain Jacobs]]
|42
|1
|2008–2017
|-
|10
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Kayode McKinnon]]
|41
|4
|2002–2012
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
| style="text-align:left;" |'''[[Omari Glasgow]]'''
|23
|37
|{{#expr:23/37 round 2}}
|2021–present 
|-
|2
| style="text-align: left;" |[[Nigel Codrington]]
|18
|26
|{{#expr:18/26 round 2}}
|2001–2010
|-
|3
| style="text-align: left;" |[[Gregory Richardson]]
|17
|46
|{{#expr:17/46 round 2}}
|2002–2019
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Anthony Abrams]]
|15
|58
|{{#expr:15/58 round 2}}
|2004–2017
|-
|5
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Osaze De Rosario]]'''
|13
|13
|{{#expr:13/13 round 2}}
|2023–present
|-
|6
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Trayon Bobb]]
|12
|51
|{{#expr:12/51 round 2}}
|2011–2023
|-
|rowspan=2|7
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Neil Danns]]
|11
|23
|{{#expr:11/23 round 2}}
|2015–2023
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Emery Welshman]]
|11
|28
|{{#expr:11/28 round 2}}
|2015–2023
|-
|rowspan=2|9
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Randolph Jerome]]
|9
|21
|{{#expr:9/21 round 2}}
|1998–2008
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Sheldon Holder]]
|9
|34
|{{#expr:9/34 round 2}}
|2011–2021
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]]
!rowspan=28|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
| [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
| colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
| colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
| colspan=8|''Did not enter''
| colspan=6|''Declined participation''
|-
| {{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
| 2 || 1 || 0 || 1 || 2 || 3
|-
| {{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
| 6 || 2 || 0 || 4 || 8 || 13
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
| 2 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 1 || 2
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy|1946}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
| 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 5
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
| 2 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 2 || 3
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
| 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1 || 8
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| colspan=8|''Suspended by FIFA''
| colspan=6|''Suspended by FIFA''
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=6 | ''Did not qualify''
| 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1 || 8
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1 || 3
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 12 || 4 || 2 || 6 || 14 || 30
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 2 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 6 || 6 
|-
| {{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
| 4 || 1 || 0 || 3 || 4 || 8
|-
| {{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
| 4 || 2 || 0 || 2 || 6 || 4
|-
| {{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
| colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
| {{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total|| || {{Tooltip|0/13|Number of tournaments qualified for}} || || || || || || || 42 || 10 || 6 || 26 || 46 || 93
|}

===CONCACAF Gold Cup===
{{main|Guyana at the CONCACAF Gold Cup}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=10|[[CONCACAF Championship]] & [[CONCACAF Gold Cup|Gold Cup]] record
! rowspan="31" |
!colspan=7|[[CONCACAF Gold Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|SLV}} [[1963 CONCACAF Championship|1963]]||colspan=9 rowspan=6|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|GUA}} [[1965 CONCACAF Championship|1965]] 
|-
|{{flagicon|HON|1949}} [[1967 CONCACAF Championship|1967]] 
|-
|{{flagicon|CRC}} [[1969 CONCACAF Championship|1969]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1971 CONCACAF Championship|1971]] 
|- 
|{{flagicon|HAI|1964}} [[1973 CONCACAF Championship|1973]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|MEX}} [[1977 CONCACAF Championship|1977]]||colspan=9 rowspan=7|''Did not qualify''
||2||1||0||1||2||3
|-
|{{flagicon|HON|1949}} [[1981 CONCACAF Championship|1981]] 
||6||2||0||4||8||13
|-
|[[1985 CONCACAF Championship|1985]] 
||2||0||1||1||1||2
|-
|[[1989 CONCACAF Championship|1989]] 
||2||0||0||2||0||5
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1991 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1991]]
||6||2||1||3||9||15
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[1993 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1993]] 
||3||1||0||2||3||7
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]] 
||2||0||0||2||0||7
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1998 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1998]]||colspan=9|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2000 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2000]]||colspan=9 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
||5||1||2||2||19||12
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2002 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2002]]
||3||2||0||1||4||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2003 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2003]]
||2||1||0||1||2||2
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2005 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2005]]||colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2007 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2007]]||colspan=9 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
|9||7||1||1||28||7
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2009]] 
|5||1||3||1||7||5
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2011]]
|3||0||1||2||1||6
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2013 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2013]]
|8||4||0||4||15||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2015 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2015]]
|3||0||1||2||0||4
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2017 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2017]]
|6||3||0||3||21||12
|-
|{{flagicon|Costa Rica}} {{flagicon|Jamaica}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]]||Group stage||13th||3||0||1||2||3||9
|[[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup squads#Guyana|Squad]]
|4||3||0||1||14||3
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2021]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|7||3||1||3||12||14
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]]
|8||3||2||3||9||17
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2025]]
|8||3||1||4||17||21
|-
!Total||Group stage||1/28||3||0||1||2||3||9||—
!93
!37
!14
!43
!172
!169
|}

===CONCACAF Nations League===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
! colspan="20" |[[CONCACAF Nations League]] record
|-
! colspan="10" |League
! rowspan="7" |
! colspan="9" |Finals
|-
!Season
!Division
!Group
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
! width="35" |{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/relegation at end of season}}
!'''Finals'''
!'''Result'''
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Squad|Finals Squad Only}}
|-
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League|2019–20]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group C|C]]
|6
|3
|1
|2
|12
|10
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2021 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2021]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="3" |''Ineligible''
|-
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group B|B]]
|6
|3
|1
|2
|8
|14
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2023 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2023]]
|-
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League|2023–24]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group D|D]]
|5
|5
|0
|0
|20
|5
|{{rise}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2024 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2024]]
|-
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League A|A]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League A#Group A|A]]
|4
|0
|1
|3
|5
|13
|{{Down}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2025 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2025]]
| colspan="8" |''Did not qualify''
|-
!Total
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!21
!11
!3
!7
!45
!42
!'''—'''
!'''Total'''
!'''0 Titles'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
|}

===Caribbean Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[CFU Championship]] & [[Caribbean Cup]] record 
!!width=1% rowspan=28|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Abbr|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Abbr|W|Won}}
!{{Abbr|D|Drawn}}
!{{Abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Abbr|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Abbr|W|Won}}
!{{Abbr|D|Drawn}}
!{{Abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1978 CFU Championship|1978]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||2||0||0||2||1||4
|-
|{{flagicon|SUR}} [[1979 CFU Championship|1979]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Puerto Rico}} [[1981 CFU Championship|1981]]
|-
|{{flagicon|French Guiana}} [[1983 CFU Championship|1983]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
||4||1||2||1||3||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Barbados}} [[1985 CFU Championship|1985]]
||2||1||1||0||1||1
|-
|{{flagicon|Martinique|snake}} [[1988 CFU Championship|1988]]
||2||0||0||2||0||5
|- 
|{{flagicon|BAR}} [[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1990 Caribbean Cup|1990]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||3||0||2||1||1||6
|-bgcolor=LemonChiffon
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1991 Caribbean Cup|1991]]||'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''4'''||'''1'''||'''0'''||'''3'''||'''4'''||'''14'''
||2||1||1||0||5||1
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1992 Caribbean Cup|1992]]||colspan=8 rowspan=9|''Did not qualify''
||3||1||1||1||4||5
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1993 Caribbean Cup|1993]]
||3||1||0||2||3||7
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1994 Caribbean Cup|1994]]
||2||0||0||2||1||4
|-
|{{flagicon|CAY}} {{flagicon|JAM}} [[1995 Caribbean Cup|1995]]
||2||0||0||2||0||7
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1996 Caribbean Cup|1996]]
||2||1||0||1||3||3
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} {{flagicon|SKN}} [[1997 Caribbean Cup|1997]]
||4||1||1||2||5||9
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} {{flagicon|TRI}} [[1998 Caribbean Cup|1998]]
||3||1||1||1||17||4
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1999 Caribbean Cup|1999]]
||2||0||1||1||2||4
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2001 Caribbean Cup|2001]]
||3||2||0||1||4||3
|-
|{{flagicon|BRB}} [[2005 Caribbean Cup|2005]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2007 Caribbean Cup|2007]]||Group stage||5th||3||1||1||1||4||5
||6||6||0||0||24||2
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||5||1||3||1||7||5
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2010 Caribbean Cup|2010]]||Group stage||8th||3||0||1||2||1||6
||6||4||1||1||9||4
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[2012 Caribbean Cup|2012]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
||6||3||0||3||11||9
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2014 Caribbean Cup|2014]]
||3||0||1||2||0||4
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2017 Caribbean Cup|2017]]
||6||3||0||3||21||12
|-
!Total||Fourth place||3/25||10||2||2||6||9||25
!71||27||15||29||122||104
|}

==Honours==
===Friendly===
* '''Martinez Shield (3)''': 1929, 1931, 1932<ref name="Martinez Shield"/>

==See also==
{{portal|Association football|Guyana}}
* [[Guyana national under-20 football team]]
* [[Guyana national under-17 football team]]
* [[Football in Guyana]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{commons category|Guyana men's national association football team|Guyana national football team}}
* {{official website|https://www.guyanafootball.org}}
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/GUY Guyana] – [[FIFA]] profile

{{Guyana national football team}}
{{Navboxes
|title=Guyana squads – CONCACAF Gold Cup
|bg = #2A936A
|fg = #FFC20E
|bordercolor = #BE1E2D
|list1=
{{Guyana squad 2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup}}
}}
{{Football in Guyana}}
{{CONCACAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Guyana}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Guyana National Football Team}}
[[Category:Guyana national football team| ]]
[[Category:Guyana national football teams| ]]
[[Category:South American national association football teams]]
[[Category:Caribbean national association football teams]]
[[Category:1905 establishments in British Guiana]]
[[Category:National sports teams established in 1905]]
